args.content: ELM-007 approved design: restructure the extension sidepanel into a chat-first single continuous conversation surface. Extend the existing conversation shell, event mapper, renderer, and live/history UX so user prompt, live reasoning summary, stage progress, inline question options, final answer, and follow-up turns appear in one stream. Preserve existing SSE transport, question submission, and history persistence contracts. Default-hide or visually demote legacy context/status/result-summary/history cards so they remain auxiliary rather than primary.

result.designSummary: ELM-007 approved design: restructure the extension sidepanel into a chat-first single continuous conversation surface. Extend the existing conversation shell, event mapper, renderer, and live/history UX so user prompt, live reasoning summary, stage progress, inline question options, final answer, and follow-up turns appear in one stream. Preserve existing SSE transport, question submission, and history persistence contracts. Default-hide or visually demote legacy context/status/result-summary/history cards so they remain auxiliary rather than primary.

args.content: ELM-007 Decision: extend extension/src/sidepanel/App.tsx as the ownership boundary for sidepanel orchestration and layout rather than introducing a new top-level page component tree. Rationale: App.tsx already owns prompt submission, runtime state merging, permission/context wiring, question answer submission, and history/rules composition, so a brownfield-safe restructuring should refactor this shell instead of replacing it.

decisions: ELM-007 Decision: extend extension/src/sidepanel/App.tsx as the ownership boundary for sidepanel orchestration and layout rather than introducing a new top-level page component tree. Rationale: App.tsx already owns prompt submission, runtime state merging, permission/context wiring, question answer submission, and history/rules composition, so a brownfield-safe restructuring should refactor this shell instead of replacing it.

args.content: ELM-007 Decision: introduce a new internal chat-stream view-model seam inside the existing conversation mapper rather than a new top-level software unit. Rationale: extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts already aggregates run events into conversation turns and reasoning summaries, but App.tsx still manually assembles prompt bubble, assistant reply, reasoning timeline, and question card as separate UI fragments. A unified ChatStreamItem-style mapper is the minimal safe seam that enables a single in-stream conversation surface.

Rationale: App.tsx already owns prompt submission, runtime state merging, permission/context wiring, question answer submission, and history/rules composition, so a brownfield-safe restructuring should refactor this shell instead of replacing it.

ELM-007 Decision: introduce a new internal chat-stream view-model seam inside the existing conversation mapper rather than a new top-level software unit. Rationale: extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts already aggregates run events into conversation turns and reasoning summaries, but App.tsx still manually assembles prompt bubble, assistant reply, reasoning timeline, and question card as separate UI fragments. A unified ChatStreamItem-style mapper is the minimal safe seam that enables a single in-stream conversation surface.

args.content: ELM-007 Decision: keep createRunEventStream, submitQuestionAnswer, questionState helpers, and IndexedDB run history contracts unchanged. Rationale: the approved requirement is a UX restructuring, not a transport or persistence redesign, and preserving these contracts reduces brownfield risk while keeping live streaming and multi-turn continuity intact.

Rationale: the approved requirement is a UX restructuring, not a transport or persistence redesign, and preserving these contracts reduces brownfield risk while keeping live streaming and multi-turn continuity intact.

args.content: ELM-007 Decision: render question options and answer interactions inline within the main chat stream instead of as a standalone QuestionCard below the timeline. Rationale: standalone question blocks break conversation continuity and conflict with the approved single-flow opencode-like interaction model.

Rationale: App.tsx already owns prompt submission, runtime state merging, permission/context wiring, question answer submission, and history/rules composition, so a brownfield-safe restructuring should refactor this shell instead of replacing it.

ELM-007 Decision: introduce a new internal chat-stream view-model seam inside the existing conversation mapper rather than a new top-level software unit. Rationale: extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts already aggregates run events into conversation turns and reasoning summaries, but App.tsx still manually assembles prompt bubble, assistant reply, reasoning timeline, and question card as separate UI fragments. A unified ChatStreamItem-style mapper is the minimal safe seam that enables a single in-stream conversation surface.

ELM-007 Decision: keep createRunEventStream, submitQuestionAnswer, questionState helpers, and IndexedDB run history contracts unchanged. Rationale: the approved requirement is a UX restructuring, not a transport or persistence redesign, and preserving these contracts reduces brownfield risk while keeping live streaming and multi-turn continuity intact.

ELM-007 Decision: render question options and answer interactions inline within the main chat stream instead of as a standalone QuestionCard below the timeline. Rationale: standalone question blocks break conversation continuity and conflict with the approved single-flow opencode-like interaction model.

args.content: ELM-007 Decision: preserve context, status, capture summary, history navigation, and rule configuration as secondary utilities that are hidden, collapsed, or mode-switched by default. Rationale: these capabilities remain operationally useful but must no longer dominate first paint or primary reading order.

tasks.title: Refactor App.tsx into a chat-first sidepanel shell for ELM-007
tasks.summary: Restructure extension/src/sidepanel/App.tsx so the conversation stream becomes the primary surface, the composer remains continuously available for follow-up questions, and legacy context/status/capture/history blocks are hidden or visually demoted by default while preserving existing orchestration and question-state wiring.

tasks.title: Add unified chat-stream view-model mapping in reasoningTimeline.ts for ELM-007
tasks.summary: Extend extension/src/sidepanel/reasoningTimeline.ts with a ChatStreamItem-style mapper that combines user prompt, reasoning summaries, stage progress, inline question payloads, final result, and error states into one ordered stream model consumable by the renderer.

tasks.title: Render inline question options and collapsible reasoning in reasoningTimelineView.tsx for ELM-007

tasks.title: Restyle sidepanel to opencode-like chat-first hierarchy in styles.css for ELM-007
tasks.summary: Update extension/src/sidepanel/styles.css to support a full-height continuous chat surface, persistent bottom composer, subdued secondary utility panels, and stronger visual hierarchy around the single conversation stream while remaining responsive in the sidepanel.

tasks.title: Align live and history conversation rendering in App.tsx/useRunHistory.ts for ELM-007
